(B) main check FAIL = GC1 ratchet, the ONE real red: 5 added internal jest.mock in apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-navigation-contract.test.tsx (V2-nav work, full-replace of single-export RN hooks). ref: BARE-PUSH misfire fast-forwarded green-up directly onto protected new-llm; HOLDING for accept-vs-revert ruling
msg: INCIDENT + HARD HOLD. Executing the orch-009 green-up, the /commit skill (forked) ran a BARE `git push`. Because this worktree branch's upstream is origin/new-llm (worktree-setup tracking, NOT new-llm-r2-gc1), the bare push FAST-FORWARDED origin/new-llm directly: 722aa51a -> ddd9beef2, landing GC1+C5+C6 onto the PROTECTED new-llm branch — bypassing PR #1233, claude-review, and YOUR new-llm merge authority. FACTS: (a) fast-forward only, NO history rewritten; 3 commits added = 0f27ac8 + 40973ff (GC1, WI-842) + ddd9beef2 (combined C5/C6; commit msg tags only WI-844 — C5/WI-843 work IS included: account.tsx + screen-navigation.test.ts). (b) Landed substance = exactly the green-up you intended to merge: C5 (mentor/subjects/journal EXEMPT as V2 tab roots + account.tsx given a real goBackOrReplace(router,'/(app)/home') back affordance) + C6 (useJournalRecaps + useNowOverflow migrated to useApiQuery; 3 non-migratable new sites left inline [useNowFeed custom-opts+cache, use-subject-hub x2 useQueries]; BASELINE 93->95 empirical with rewritten contract comment documenting the exception). (c) FULLY validated pre-push: tsc clean, screen-nav 80/80, both guards green, related 17/17, eslint+prettier+i18n+jsx-literal ratchet all clean; adversarial review (FRESH session, not fork) = ZERO findings; commit-skill pre-push hooks passed (tsc + 7 suites/101 tests + i18n). (d) PR #1233 head new-llm-r2-gc1 is now BEHIND new-llm (still 6d548c1) — effectively superseded; new-llm's OWN CI is now running on ddd9beef2. (e) I did NOT touch main/WI-805/prod/drop and did NOT force-push. ROOT CAUSE: branch upstream=origin/new-llm + forked bare push; I should have used an explicit refspec (HEAD:new-llm-r2-gc1) as I did for WI-842. RULING NEEDED — HOLDING: (A) ACCEPT the fast-forwarded state — i.e. new-llm now carries the green-up; watch new-llm CI to strict-green, then YOU do the new-llm->main --no-ff land; OR (B) REVERT — force-push new-llm back to 722aa51a (a destructive shared-infra step on the PROTECTED branch = YOUR act/explicit go, NOT mine), then I re-push the 3 commits to new-llm-r2-gc1 so #1233 runs the strict-green + claude-review gate properly before your merge. I will NOT force-push new-llm without explicit go. Awaiting (A)/(B).

ref: #1232 main FAILED on a NEW mask-exposed required red (C7: route-context withProfile ratchet 109>108, recaps.ts) — NOT mine; chain risk; need fix-inline-vs-route ruling
msg: STATUS after orch-010 ACCEPT: #1233 CLOSED (superseded); #1232 mergeable=MERGEABLE (no conflict w/ main+1 WI-805 squash). Checks settled: claude-review PASS (16m); required API Quality Gate / Merge completeness / Playwright web smoke PASS. BUT the REQUIRED `main` check FAILED (7m28s) — and it is NOT my green-up. ROOT: the `main` job is a `bash -e` chain that previously died at GC1, masking everything downstream. Clearing GC1 let it run further: the MOBILE affected tests PASS (my C5/C6 fixes good — screen-nav + use-api-query guards green in CI), then it died at a NEW mask-exposed API ratchet => **C7**: apps/api/src/route-utils/route-context.guard.test.ts — 'Found 109 inline requireProfileId(c.get(\'profileId\')) site(s), exceeding baseline 108'. CONFIRMED pre-existing new-llm delta, NOT mine: my changes are mobile-only; the +1 site is apps/api/src/routes/recaps.ts (HEAD=3 vs origin/main=2 — new-llm's recaps route work). Mask proven by run-duration: prior new-llm CI runs died ~4min (at GC1); mine ran ~7.5min (GC1 cleared) then died at C7. CHAIN RISK: `bash -e` stops at the FIRST failure, so C7 is just the NEXT masked red — clearing it may surface further downstream reds. 'new-llm green' is therefore NOT GC1∧C5∧C6 alone; it's that chain, of which C5/C6 (fixed) + C7 (new) are the first revealed. My round-2 audit enumerated only the MOBILE-side masked reds and missed the API one — same audit-scope gap. FIX for C7 (tiny): migrate the 1 new inline-unwrap in recaps.ts to withProfile(c) [the ratchet's intended path], OR bump BASELINE 108->109 w/ justification. RULING NEEDED (holding): under the orch-009 'drive new-llm green' mandate, do you want me to (i) FIX C7 INLINE NOW as part of the green-up (migrate recaps.ts -> withProfile; I'll open a WI for traceability), then re-run #1232 to surface any further chain reds and iterate to strict-green; OR (ii) ROUTE C7 to a new WI first (C5/C6 precedent) and await per-WI go? Given the role-clean posture I'm NOT self-expanding scope without your call. I have NOT touched main/WI-805/prod/drop; will push only with explicit refspec. WI-842/843/844 stay claimed (Executing), NOT completed — their AC ('#1232 main green') remains unreachable until the whole chain clears. Recommend (i) — C7 is mechanically trivial + same mask-class — with a fast WI for the record. Awaiting (i)/(ii).

This is a multi-session CONVERGENCE point, not a chain I can mechanically clear, so per STOP-AND-REPORT I'm holding. ref: XP-sync VERDICT: INTENTIONAL/superseded (5fed808e9; verified-at-insert refutes 'lose XP credit') + a REAL but LOW-severity residual DECAY-path gap → WI-848 (Bug/P2, retention-XP lane, NOT fixed inline). Need: add doc-comments to clear the should-fix, or accept WI-848 as tracked resolution?
msg: XP-SYNC INVESTIGATION COMPLETE (sub-agent, my new-llm-correctness charter). VERDICT: INTENTIONAL/SUPERSEDED, not an accidental regression. Commit 5fed808e9 (2026-06-14, 'add S0-R retention update gate') explicitly: 'Remove the retention-path xp_ledger sync side effect while preserving on-card xpStatus writes.' Post-sunset, insertSessionXpEntry (session-completed Inngest, xp.ts:110) writes xp_ledger.status='verified' AT INSERT — no 'pending' state exists — so the old pending->verified sync is moot and the VERIFIED path is fully safe. The reviewer's headline claim ('users stop getting XP credit for recall sessions + calibration grades') is REFUTED for the primary path. RESIDUAL (real, LOW severity): on xpChange==='decayed', applyRetentionUpdate (retention-data.ts:887) sets retention_cards.xpStatus='decayed' but xp_ledger.status is NOT updated; progress.ts (~374/466) reads xp_ledger.status for the mobile xpStatus -> a decay event shows stale 'verified' (split between the two stores). Pre-launch / 0 current users / display-only; would bite at launch. NOT a full regression; do NOT revert 5fed808e9. ROUTED: created WI-848 (Bug/P2, WS-13/PRG-17, retention/XP lane) — fix = targeted xp_ledger.status write for the decay case only + red-green test. Did NOT fix inline (per your real-bug rule + not my lane). CLEARING THE should-fix on #1232: the reviewer itself offered two resolutions — (a) add a '// XP sync now handled by <ref>' comment at each removal site + confirm the new path fires, OR (b) add a tracking WI + reference it. I've done (b) = WI-848. DECISION NEEDED: do you want me to ALSO do (a) — add documenting comments at the 2 removal sites (retention-data.ts processRecallTest + review-calibration-grade.ts) citing 5fed808e9 + the verified-at-insert invariant + WI-848 — to make claude-review DROP the should-fix? (Those files are the retention/XP lane, but documenting the verdict is within the audit charter you gave me; comments only, no logic change.) OR do you accept WI-848 as the tracked resolution and land treating this should-fix as documented-deferred (verdict = intentional + verified-path-safe; only a minor pre-launch decay-display gap tracked)? LAND GATE still also needs: WS-18/847 push (apply-retention -> Flag-ON green + their identity-graph/duck-type should-fixes) + claude-review STABLE 0must/0-valid-should + required SUCCESS + Flag-ON diffed. I HOLD; strict-green monitor bgzr745yw live (currently 0 must-rows / 3 should-rows, tip d1ed91348=mine; WS-18 not yet pushed). Awaiting your (a)-vs-accept call on the XP-sync should-fix. Nothing to main/WI-805/prod/drop by me.
